title: Aliasing, Effective Type, Provenance, and Type Punning · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted defined binary decoder with provenance notes, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in defined binary decoder with provenance notes and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Access allocated and copied storage through types permitted by effective-type rules or defined character-byte paths.
Preserve optimizer-visible non-aliasing rules and test any deliberate compiler-extension policy.
Use memcpy into a live destination object for representation transfer and validate the resulting value domain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Provenance Ts Review
steps.instruction:
Order the Provenance Ts Review evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Provenance Ts Review.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Provenance Ts Review assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Provenance Ts Review, keep Provenance Ts Review connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug native-systems team handles memory case 7471. Produce bounded C evidence to decode representation bytes through a defined copy; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply ISO/IEC TS 6010 provenance concepts as reviewed guidance without mislabeling them as universally implemented C23 behavior.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Provenance Ts Review, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: The provenance technical specification is already identical to every production compiler memory model.
